title: Dungeons and Dogs: Lost Brothers
blurb: Adult R-rated pulp. Three amnesiac brothers and a dog wake in the Neon Wilderland — cyberpunk steel under a Middle-earth canopy, spirits in the fog, dragons on the ridgeline. Helix Dominion sells names; Project Pale wiped theirs. Chapters 1–6: Woods Without Names through House of Seals. Chapters 7–9 still await the next rewrite pass.

encounterCadence.note: Engine-side: roll next random ambush after 10–20 frames advance; prefer chapter enemyThemes.

chapters.title: Woods Without Names
chapters.tagline: Three brothers wake lost among neon ruins and ancient trees — no idea who they are.

chapters.title: Stim Dust Marches
chapters.tagline: Warrants pay in stims and scars while Quill teaches steel that answers to no seal.

chapters.title: SKU Trace
chapters.tagline: An under-skin barcode leads east; friends and hunters argue over who owns the men who woke blank.

chapters.title: Chromeveil River
chapters.tagline: Barges haul chemical cargo and spirit-caged freight across a river the freeholds refuse to see.

chapters.title: Helix Gate
chapters.tagline: Director Vale’s border keeps smile for coin and drones for mercy; Lyra is somewhere past the smoke.

chapters.title: House of Seals
chapters.tagline: Guest rooms overlook yards of rings and numbers; friendship here is whispered at risk of skin.

chapters.title: Blood Mandolin
chapters.tagline: Candlemire's sport pits fighters under lanterns while a mandolin keeps time with screams.

chapters.title: Liberation March
chapters.tagline: Cages open. Brands break. The field that grew cane learns a different crop: uprising.

chapters.title: Free Horizon
chapters.tagline: Roads west remember every foot that left a collar behind — and ask what freedom costs next.

nodes.title: Wake Cold
nodes.body: You come to face-down in pine needles that glow a sick biolume green. Rain tastes like ozone and iron. Two other men — your brothers, somehow, even if none of you can prove it — sit against a shattered neon waymarker humming in Elvish and static. No names. No phones. Only a skyline of future towers beyond the trees, and something winged blotting the moon.

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Empty Pockets
nodes.body: Pockets hold chrome knives, a cracked plasma pack, a charm carved from bone that still whispers when you shake it. The woods are Middle-earth wrong: ancient oaks wrapped in fiber-optic vines, a stream running silver like coolant. One brother swears he hears a dragon’s radio-chatter. Another laughs once, then stops, because laughing without a name feels like theft.

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Who Are We
nodes.body: You try the usual inventory of self — job, city, last fight — and bounce off blank. The trees watch like old spirits wearing new cameras. Somewhere deeper, a synth-choir and a war-horn argue over the same note. You three decide the only true thing left: stick together until the woods spit out an answer or a corpse.

nodes.title: Trail Tax in Chrome
nodes.body: Three scavengers block a root-bridge with a trip-laser and a grin. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — taxes travelers in coin, batteries, or blood. A lean stranger in a ruined grey hat watches from the bank, hand near a crossbow bolt wired to a microcell he has not spent yet. Spirits flicker in the mist like bad holograms.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the bridge with cells and coin; save steel for worse.

outcome.text: They salute with stolen manners. The grey-hatted stranger mutters, “Inflation,” and does not leave.

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show chrome teeth and the stranger’s wired bolt.

outcome.text: Laser drops. One kid whispers, “The lost brothers walk.” Word travels usefully — maybe too usefully.

choices.label: Trade a woods story
choices.approach: Sell them a tale sharp enough to buy safe passage twice.

success.text: They point you to a dry cache under the bridge roots — amateurs with useful greed. The stranger tips his ruined hat a fraction.

fail.text: They laugh wrong. The stranger pays a copper anyway and files the insult for later.

nodes.title: The Stranger's Ugly Question
nodes.body: The grey-hatted stranger steps close enough that whiskey and ozone ride his breath. He asks — not unkind, not soft — whether you three want to be brothers who hunt, or hunters who happen to be brothers. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears. “And don’t you damn quit either way. Woods like these eat quitters and sell the bones as charms.”

choices.label: Brothers who hunt
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: kinship first, the kill second.

outcome.text: “Then we hunt like guests,” he drawls. “Violent guests. Stubborn ones. If the woods let you keep each other.”

choices.label: Hunters who are brothers
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the men — and the bond.

outcome.text: He almost smiles with the scarred eye. “Honest. Ugly. Useful.” A drone-spirit circles once and leaves.

choices.label: Neither — find our names
choices.approach: Refuse his categories; chase memory before identity.

outcome.text: “Categories are cages,” he allows. “Fine. Be a vow with three blades — if the forest takes your price.”

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Signal Weather
nodes.body: Heat makes the biolume itch like unfinished code. Eyes linger on your blank faces the way buyers linger on mule teeth — and servers linger on fresh accounts. The stranger packs silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous — and waits for the woods to decide if you are prey, players, or both.

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Powder Prayer
nodes.body: A child’s chalk name on a fence post has already been scrubbed to static. The stranger says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” Spirit-fog still clings; whatever comes next will have to wash harder than rain — chrome, prayer, or both.

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Stubborn Mile
nodes.body: Scavengers sell water and buy stories about nameless men who would not quit. You set your pace like brothers owed a past and too stubborn to renegotiate. The stranger lights a match off a warrant nail wired into a data-spike, drinks, and looks almost human — almost ready to spend truth.

nodes.title: The Binder Arrives
nodes.body: The lean marshal tips his ruined hat like a man haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — freehold binder, warrant-runner, one scarred eye that never quite closes on a lie — does not buy your papers. He buys your attention. “Pretty muscle. Ugly amnesia. I’ll take the stubborn set. Woods east of here eat solo heroes.”

nodes.title: Neon Glade: Ring Memory
nodes.body: Wind carries iron smell and dragon-static farther than any honest voice. Somewhere east, old spirits still invent people into inventory — and new networks invent people into debt. Continue — soft roads make soft seals again, even in chrome.

nodes.title: Neon Glade: East Debt
nodes.body: Overseer laughter and server fans carry farther than any hymn on this road. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. The empty place where your names should be weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

nodes.title: Quill Names the Work
nodes.body: “I walk with lost men who can ruin the things that lost them,” Quill says, flask catching firelight and neon. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load — and if you quit mid-woods, I’ll leave you for the crows with a receipt.” You three load — plasma, steel, and a charm that still whispers.

nodes.title: Vern's Joke
nodes.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid stim-tax and burned freeholds — half cyberpunk bar story, half orc-raid yarn. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty, then mutters that hell of a comedian still makes hell of a warrant.

nodes.title: Dust Mile
nodes.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. You check for a brand, a barcode, a seal scar — and find only the itch of missing names. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

nodes.title: Road Wargs Ambush
nodes.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. A hanged poster of three blank faces lies about the eyes — always the eyes. You walk because the alternative still smells like rust — and quit is not a word you own. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel and future scrap; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Road Wargs falls. The woods taste like iron, ozone, and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, spirit-noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Memory first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Trail Hygiene
nodes.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where a SKU poster used to hang. One brother rolls a shoulder that still remembers chrome manners. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten in blood or static.

nodes.title: Paper Callsigns
nodes.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across a chrome-scarred wagon board and uncorks a dented flask that smells like stims and worse honesty. Conditional freedom: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry, and I don’t ride with quitters.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

outcome.text: Quill smiles without warmth. “Good. The woods hate empty hands, full sermons, and soft guts.”

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

success.text: He softens half a degree and takes a pull. “Lyra Vale. Sold east years ago — sealed operator, pretty SKU. Helix keeps prettier ledgers and uglier guests. You don’t quit easy — fine. Neither does that name.”

fail.text: Quill’s scarred eye narrows. “Earn the damn right to ask.” He still pushes the papers toward you.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys sealed people for worse work.

success.text: No twitch — only tired calculation and a flask that never quite empties. Dangerous, yes. Aligned, for now. He notices you looking and tips his hat at the insult.

fail.text: You misread courtesy for cruelty. Quill shrugs. “Everyone does, once.” He hands you a short sword and a look that says don’t quit on the first mile.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Old hunger is not food. It is the itch where a true name should sit. The dog eats first; brothers argue second; Quill drinks like punctuation.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Callsign Thoughts
nodes.body: Cage-static still squeaks in the memory of your spine. One brother keeps counsel; Quill keeps cartridges and contempt. Callsigns feel safer than true names — and the spirits agree.

nodes.title: Pale-Mark Burial
nodes.body: Quill has you scratch Pale-Mark into roadside dirt and kick it apart. “Numbers are cages that fit in a mouth,” he says. “Answer to a callsign — Anchor, Edge, Signal — never inventory. And don’t you quit back into a digit.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: SKU Weather
nodes.body: Under-skin itch flares when drones pass. The dog growls at empty air. Quill says that is how Project Pale says hello without introducing itself.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Steel Practice
nodes.body: Quill’s ruined hat casts a verdict-shaped shadow. His crossbow speaks better than sermons. You practice kill-angles until muscle memory answers for the blank mind.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Night Watch
nodes.body: Two riders tip hats too friendly for the hour. One brother counts exits without moving his head. The dog’s ears invent enemies before the trees do.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Iron Humor
nodes.body: the nameless woods mist is gone; neon fog replaces it. Quill drawls that paper freedom first — real freedom files later — don’t quit in between. East keeps yards that have not yet learned uprising arithmetic.

nodes.title: Seal Cut Loose
nodes.body: You find a scavenger’s neural collar half-melted into pine bark — Dominion property still blinking a weak green. Quill pries it with a hooked key that smells of rust, stim-sweat, and other people’s surrender. He tosses the ring into biolume mud like trash that once owned a man — then grinds it under his heel, just to be thorough.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Seal Echo
nodes.body: Seal echo is a ghost in the throat: old obedience trying to stand up. You spit it into biolume mud and load the plasma pack.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human — almost ready to spend truth about Project Pale.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Hard Mercy
nodes.body: Steel rides light; the flask in Quill’s coat does not. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. The woods shrink behind you like a cult losing membership.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Scav Bargain
nodes.body: A scavenger offers stim tabs for a true name. You offer him a bruise and a rumor instead. Quill tips him a copper for the geography hidden in the greed.

nodes.title: Road Wargs Ambush
nodes.body: The seal-scar itches when scrap-wagons pass — muscle memory of inventory. The next mile pretends to be empty and lies about being empty. Somewhere a seal-yard waits to learn a different inventory: revolt. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Quill's Aside
nodes.body: Quill’s aside is never soft: “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” Spirit-fog still clings; whatever comes next washes harder than rain.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Neon Wind
nodes.body: Neon wind tastes like coolant and pine. A winged silhouette crosses the moon again — drone or wyrm, same omen: something big owns the skyline.

nodes.title: First Mile Free
nodes.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose east, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch neon ruins dwindle and look personally offended. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners — and a microcell crossbow for a chaperone.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Binder Math
nodes.body: Binder math: three stubborn blanks plus one dog plus one drunk marshal equals a hunting party the woods notice. Quill does not apologize for the arithmetic.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Flask Sermon
nodes.body: Quill’s flask is chemical truth serum with worse manners. One sip and brother-sync feels like a remembered fight. Two sips and names start knocking.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Camp Gossip
nodes.body: Somebody swears Helix Spire hangs rings by size, not by name. Quill drinks past it, scarred eye fixed east, and says only, “Then we learn loft latches.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Seal Weather
nodes.body: Seal weather rolls in like debt. Neural collars chirp in scav packs; spirit meters blink green for clean product. You are not clean. Good.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Powder Prayer
nodes.body: Powder prayer is loading a plasma pack while pretending it is faith. The dog watches like a priest that bites.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Stubborn Mile
nodes.body: Quill lights a match off a warrant nail wired into a data-spike, drinks, and looks almost human — almost ready to spend truth.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Meter Memory
nodes.body: Wind carries dragon-static and spirit-meter hum. Continue — soft roads make soft seals again, even in chrome.

nodes.title: Glade Raiders
nodes.body: Three glade raiders peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the spirit-cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned with interest. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and drawls, “Show them the paperwork is yours — and that the warranty expired.”

outcome.text: Glade Raider Reclaimer falls. The woods taste like iron, ozone, and relief.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Quiet Counsel
nodes.body: One brother hears dragon radio-chatter in the canopy. Another laughs once, then stops. The third checks the dog’s eyes for answers.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Trail Hygiene
nodes.body: You scrape scavenger grease off a chrome knife and call it hygiene. The woods keep receipts in fiber-vine and bone charms. East smells like ozone and unfinished names.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets that lie for a living. A road widow sells directions and warnings in the same sentence. The nameless woods shrink behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Callsign Thoughts
nodes.body: You try on words like coats: Anchor, Edge, Signal. None fit until violence makes them stick. Quill refuses to baptize you; the woods already did.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: SKU Weather
nodes.body: Under-skin itch flares when drones pass. The dog growls at empty air. Quill says that is how Project Pale says hello without introducing itself.

nodes.title: Quill's Lesson
nodes.body: “Steel answers seal-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt like a clerk correcting a sum that insulted him. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage — then takes a pull. “Rage is fine. Just don’t let it hold the damn map. And don’t quit mid-swing.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Steel Practice
nodes.body: Chrome blade, plasma pack, bone charm that whispers — drill until the three of you move like one wetwork cell that forgot its contract.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Night Watch
nodes.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts in neon and sap.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Iron Humor
nodes.body: Distant stim fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Vern’s ledger scrap laughs from Quill’s pocket like a bad friend. Iron humor is all you’ve got left that still cuts.

nodes.title: Warrant Grammar
nodes.body: Quill makes you read a blank warrant aloud until the clauses stop sounding like mercy — alive-if-possible, dead-if-necessary, property-of-no-corp once the seal dries. “Paper is a leash that points both ways,” he drawls. “Hold your end. Don’t let Vale’s clerks invent the other.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Brother Aside
nodes.body: One brother tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Neon-scav spit misses by manners, not mercy. Paper in your coat weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Quill's Aside
nodes.body: He talks to the flask more than to you. The flask answers in stim-heat and ugly honesty. You listen anyway — mentors this rotten usually know the road.

nodes.title: Seal-Yard Rumor
nodes.body: A peddler swears Helix Spire hangs rings by size, not by name — and that sealed operators get loft windows so buyers can hear the spirit-signal before they bid. Quill does not drink to that. He drinks past it, scarred eye fixed east, and says only, “Then we learn loft latches.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Powder Prayer
nodes.body: You pray with chrome and stubbornness. The woods answer with fog that remembers your faces better than you do.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Meter Memory
nodes.body: Meter memory: a tick behind the eyes when someone says a true name nearby. None of you have one yet. The dog does — and will not share.

nodes.title: Campfire Question
nodes.body: Night fire snaps under fiber-vine oaks. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants, spikes it from the flask, and asks what you will do if Lyra Vale is already broken past rescue — sealed, wiped, or worse. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots. His scarred eye does not blink.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

outcome.text: Quill nods once. “Stubborn miracles. I hate miracles. I’ll still pack for them — and for you not quitting.”

choices.label: Then Helix Spire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Director Vale still owns the debt.

outcome.text: Quill’s eyes glitter. “Justice with teeth. I can work with that — carefully, and drunk enough to be honest.”

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the seal-yard is real.

outcome.text: “Wise or evasive — same coin until spent,” Quill murmurs. “Don’t spend it drunk. That’s my job.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Hunger arrives wearing stim-sweat and old fight memory. Quill passes the flask once — chemical truth, not kindness. Your stomach remembers rations you cannot name.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Callsign Thoughts
nodes.body: A freehold stamp would make you legal. A callsign makes you hunt. You choose the second and pretend it was always the plan.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: SKU Weather
nodes.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Quill mutters law under his breath like a man arguing with God and winning on technicalities. SKU weather rolls east — barcodes in the fog.

nodes.title: Warrant One
nodes.body: A freehold burned for unpaid stim-tribute; the killer rides with a seal that matches Vale’s outer SKUs like a signature on arson. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. “Alive if possible,” Quill says. “Dead if the world insists on being itself.”

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. Warrant weather means someone east still thinks you are property with legs.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Hard Mercy
nodes.body: Hard mercy is leaving a scavenger breathing with a warning carved into his chrome. Soft mercy is a sermon. You choose hard.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Scav Bargain

nodes.body: one brother roofs. A child’s doll face-down in soot. Tracks lead into scrub where wargs like soft meat and softer excuses. Somewhere ahead, Vern laughs at a joke only hunters and monsters find funny — and a winged shadow crosses the burn-scar once, ambiguous as a threat.

nodes.body: Stim sermon, whiskey chorus. He says Project Pale is a rumor with teeth. You feel the teeth in your blank spots.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Camp Gossip
nodes.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. A burned SKU seal on a fence rail dares you to look away. The road bills in blood, dust, and unfinished names.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Seal Weather
nodes.body: Heat makes the biolume itch like unfinished code. Eyes linger on blank faces the way buyers linger on mule teeth — and servers linger on fresh accounts.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Powder Prayer
nodes.body: You pray with chrome and stubbornness. The woods answer with fog that remembers your faces better than you do.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Stubborn Mile
nodes.body: Scavengers sell water and buy stories about nameless men who would not quit. You set your pace like brothers owed a past and too stubborn to renegotiate.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Meter Memory
nodes.body: Meter memory: a tick behind the eyes when someone says a true name nearby. None of you have one yet. The dog does — and will not share.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: East Debt
nodes.body: Quill points east like a man accusing weather. Debt accumulates in silence. Brothers accumulate in violence.

nodes.title: Spirit Hounds
nodes.body: Two spirit-hounds burst from thorn — half warg, half signal-ghost, eyes reflecting fire they did not start and do not regret. Quill covers the left; yours is the right throat — if you still remember what cages taught about hesitation.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Spirit-Hound Pair falls. The woods taste like iron, ozone, and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Dust Mile
nodes.body: Dust mile tastes like stim residue and pine ash. Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps. Quit is not a word you own.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Quiet Counsel
nodes.body: You decide the only true thing left: stick together until the woods spit out an answer or a corpse. Quietly. Violently if needed.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Trail Hygiene
nodes.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where a SKU poster used to hang. One brother rolls a shoulder that still remembers chrome manners. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten in blood or static.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Hunger arrives wearing stim-sweat and old fight memory. Quill passes the flask once — chemical truth, not kindness. Your stomach remembers rations you cannot name.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Callsign Thoughts
nodes.body: A freehold stamp would make you legal. A callsign makes you hunt. You choose the second and pretend it was always the plan.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: SKU Weather
nodes.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Quill mutters law under his breath like a man arguing with God and winning on technicalities. SKU weather rolls east — barcodes in the fog.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Steel Practice
nodes.body: Chrome blade, plasma pack, bone charm that whispers — drill until the three of you move like one wetwork cell that forgot its contract.

nodes.title: Vern Cornered
nodes.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse — comedy the forest did not request. He begs for Director Vale’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with operator SKUs. Lyra Vale’s sits three lines down, sold as “sealed song-op, unbroken.” Vern smiles like that should buy him sunrise.

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

outcome.text: Vern weeps gratitude he does not deserve. Quill stamps ALIVE like it hurts. The ledger page is yours.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

outcome.text: The forest swallows the sound. Quill files the warrant CLOSED and does not congratulate you.

choices.label: Press for Helix routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

success.text: Vern sketches fords and bribe posts. “Vale smiles when he sells people,” he spits. “You’ll hate how pretty the Spire is.”

fail.text: Vern faints before finishing. You still have the ledger shred, a headache, and Quill’s unimpressed silence.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Night Watch
nodes.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. A neon-scav spit misses your boots by manners, not mercy. Night watch means listening for spirit-fog and chrome feet.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Iron Humor
nodes.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. You practice not answering to numbers. Quill’s joke lands like a bolt — ugly, useful.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Seal Echo
nodes.body: Somewhere a spirit meter ticks like a second heart. Quill says ignore it until it starts naming you. Then kill whatever is speaking.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Warrant Weather
nodes.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. Warrant weather means someone east still thinks you are property with legs.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Hard Mercy
nodes.body: Hard mercy is leaving a scavenger breathing with a warning carved into his chrome. Soft mercy is a sermon. You choose hard.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Scav Bargain
nodes.body: Spirit-fog holograms flicker prices over your heads. Nobody buys. Everybody watches. The dog marks a tree like a treaty.

nodes.title: Road Wargs Ambush
nodes.body: Last stretch of nameless woods before freehold dust. Road wargs try one more tax in teeth. Quill does not negotiate with animals that already know your scent.

nodes.title: Name on Paper
nodes.body: You say Lyra Vale aloud until the vowels stop shaking and start sounding like a plan. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freehold town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine, powder, stim residue, and unfinished debts. Behind you, the Woods Without Names keep your blank faces like trophies.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Quill's Aside
nodes.body: “I walk with lost men who can ruin the things that lost them,” he repeats, like liturgy with better ammo.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Neon Wind
nodes.body: Wind carries iron smell and dragon-static farther than any honest voice. Somewhere east, old spirits invent people into inventory — and new networks invent people into debt.

nodes.title: Woods Without Names: Binder Math
nodes.body: He tallies ammo, stim tabs, and lies you still believe about yourselves. The sum is ugly. The sum walks east.

nodes.title: Dustmarch Bridge
nodes.body: Dustmarch’s bridge exacts a toll in stories, not coins — and they smell a lie the way dogs smell fear. You tell enough of the Woods Without Names to pass, and hide enough of Helix Spire to sleep. Quill buys ink, ball, and powder like a man preparing a small, personally motivated war.

nodes.title: Tavern Odds
nodes.body: A Dustmarch bookmaker offers odds on whether you’ll die before Helix Gate or after. Quill wants silence. The room wants a show. Your callsign itches like a tell.

choices.label: Drink water; ignore the circus
choices.approach: Refuse the economy of your death.

outcome.text: Boring wins. Quill tips the barman for the quiet.

choices.label: Bet on after
choices.approach: Make the room fund your survival myth.

outcome.text: Coin hits wood. “After,” you say. Someone cheers wrong.

choices.label: Correct the bookmaker’s manners
choices.approach: Fear teaches faster than odds.

success.text: He refunds half the room and learns new theology.

fail.text: A bottle finds your cheek. Quill ends the math with a bolt tip.

nodes.title: Flask Catechism
nodes.body: Quill offers the flask and a question with teeth: when a warrant names a man who once wore a neural collar like the itch under your skin, do you hesitate? His scarred eye waits. The paddock wind does not. Stim Dust listens like a congregation with bad manners.

choices.label: Hesitate — then decide
choices.approach: Mercy is not quit; it is timing.

outcome.text: “Timing,” Quill allows. “Just don’t dress it up as poetry.”

choices.label: No hesitation
choices.approach: Paper freedom means finishing the page.

outcome.text: He nods once. “Ugly. Useful. Don’t enjoy it.”

choices.label: Refuse the flask; keep the question
choices.approach: Stay sharp; Quill can drink for both of you.

outcome.text: “Smart,” he mutters, drinking your share. “Stubborn too. Good.”

flagEchoes.line: Hope still rides your shoulder like a stubborn second callsign seal.
You still refuse to decide Lyra Vale’s ending in advance — Quill calls it nerve.

Then hill goblins — scrap-clan steel and stolen stim packs — force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

nodes.title: Which Warrant?
nodes.body: Three warrants hang like wet laundry: a goblin raid-chief, a barge cutter on the Chromeveil feeder, and a knight who sold villagers under Vale’s quiet seal. Quill lets you choose the first debt to collect. “Pick the one that keeps you sleeping,” he says. “Or the one that doesn’t. Both teach.”

outcome.text: Quill packs snares. “Hills first. Rivers remember — and so do I.”

choices.label: Hunt the barge cutter
choices.approach: Follow water toward Helix’s quiet chemical supply.

outcome.text: “Rivers teach patience,” Quill says, already smelling mud and worse math.

choices.approach: Strike the soft armor of Vale’s respectables.

outcome.text: Quill’s smile thins. “Careful. Knights have friends with seals and soft hands.”

nodes.title: Free or Follow?
nodes.body: You can cut canvas tonight and spill rings into mud — noisy mercy — or track the barge to its Helix drop and learn routes. Quill’s face is a hung jury. Complicity tastes like river water either way.

choices.label: Cut the canvas; free who you can
choices.approach: Immediate liberation; accept the horn that follows.

outcome.text: Mud takes chains; alarms take the night. You run with wet lungs and cleaner hands.

choices.label: Track the drop point
choices.approach: Intelligence first; rescue with a map.

outcome.text: You learn landing codes and a steward’s soft knock. Quill files both.

choices.label: Bribe a poleman

success.text: He names Lyra Vale’s sale lot and looks away forever.

fail.text: He takes coin and shouts anyway. Spears bloom in reed.
